text: The respondent executed synchronized buy-sell orders to create artificial trading volume, violating SEBI PFUTP Regulations 2003.
The accused person, in possession of unpublished price-sensitive information regarding the merger, traded in the company's shares through a third party.
The data controller shared personal data of EU residents with third parties without obtaining explicit consent, breaching GDPR Article 6.
The broker failed to segregate client funds from its own proprietary funds, in violation of SEBI Broker Regulations.
The company did not file the required disclosures with the stock exchange within the prescribed 24-hour timeline under SEBI LODR.
The listed entity disclosed all material price-sensitive events on the exchange portal within the stipulated period under SEBI LODR Regulations.
The mutual fund disclosed its complete portfolio on the AMFI website within the prescribed timelines as required by SEBI regulations.
The data controller obtained freely given, specific, and informed consent from all data subjects before processing their personal data.
The company appointed a Data Protection Officer and maintained a comprehensive Record of Processing Activities as required by GDPR Article 37.
All KYC documentation was verified and updated within the period prescribed by SEBI, with complete audit trails maintained.
